settings.title: Five Signs an Email Is Trying to Trick You
settings.excerpt: Most breaches we clean up started with one convincing message, and the warning signs are easier to spot than you think.
settings.body:
The most expensive incidents we handle rarely begin with clever hacking. They begin with a member of staff clicking a link in an email that looked entirely normal.

Train your team to pause when they see any of these.

  • An urgent demand to act right now or lose access
  • A sender address that is almost right but slightly off
  • A request to move money or share a password
  • Spelling that is close but not quite professional
  • A link whose real destination does not match the text

When something feels off, the safest move is to stop and check by phone. A thirty-second call has saved several of our clients from a very bad week.
